title: rottlera muricata
intro: Rottlera muricata, also known as the "Dhup" tree, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, particularly in India, Sri Lanka, and parts of Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities. The health benefits of Rottlera muricata include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antifungal agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, and its extracts are also used in modern medicine to treat various health conditions.
